g.text: ╾──── COVERAGE PREFLIGHT ────╼
▌ UPDATE COVERAGE
package installed tools only
version before current run
purpose Preflight coverage map that explains what update-all will handle and what remains intentionally
manual.
Plain English: update-all updates the common workstation things it can safely detect.
It calls first-party/package-manager commands; it never invents a private updater or silently edits projects.
Secret safety: updater logs are redacted for common token/key shapes, and internal shell calls avoid shell startup
files.
apt / dpkg will check OS packages and package database health
snap apps will check snap refresh
flatpak apps skip flatpak not installed
Node runtime will check user-local Node runtime when update-node exists
Corepack / pnpm / yarn will check package-manager shims and activated managers
npm globals will check npm itself and globally installed npm CLIs
Claude Code CLI will check claude update
Codex CLI will check codex update
Homebrew/Linuxbrew will check brew update, tap trust preflight, brew upgrade
Bun runtime will check bun upgrade
uv tools will check uv self/tool updates
pipx apps will check pipx upgrade-all
Rust toolchains skip rustup not installed
Cargo-installed CLIs skip cargo-install-update not installed
GitHub extensions will check gh extension upgrade --all
editor extensions skip code/codium not installed
Linux firmware will check fwupdmgr update, prompt-gated
project repositories manual manual by design; each repo needs tests and rollback context
active agent runtimes manual manual by design; Hermes/OpenClaw-style stacks should not be surprise-upgraded
containers/images manual manual by design; updating images can break running services
conda environments manual manual by design; environment solves are project-specific
LLM weights manual manual by design; downloads are large and workload-specific
shell profiles manual never sourced for update logic; do not store raw secrets in .bashrc
result OK
note installed safe surfaces are updated; project-specific stacks stay explicit
